mission: Prove that a small, agent-friendly yard scenario can reveal hidden infrastructure, record meaningful evidence, and support a real drainage/repair round without losing the larger world-first identity.

portfolio_context.note: Point-in-time reminder, not a scope change: this project can be described as a simulation-backed agent-testing harness where puzzle-matrix scenarios, role-differentiated tester strategies, evidence capture, and human adjudication reveal hidden-system behavior.
portfolio_context.guardrail: Keep the project fun, systems-first, and focused on one legible replayable round; do not turn it into an enterprise AI demo.

current_focus: session boundary instead of god-object main.cpp

scenario.name: Granny's Yard drainage round
scenario.objective: Deliver enough water to the north bed without soaking the cellar edge or softening the yard path.
scenario.hidden_dependency: a buried cross-link from the drain mouth toward the cellar-side runoff path

packet_contains: recent events (tokenized, first no-op only until the world changes)
command_format: "action_id": any legal action id from the current packet
"target": optional visible target id (use null for area-level actions)
"selection_x"/"selection_y"/"selection_z": optional block selection coordinates (required together for "select_block")

notes: recent notes instead of raw event spam
agent packet remains available for copy/export

evidence_model.score_policy: system records facts; host awards points manually later

implementation_notes: Keep the current D3D12 grass-field harness.
Use shared sim/playtest code for scenario truth and agent packets.
Treat the viewport as presentation, not the subject.
Keep shader/render novelty on pause unless it directly unblocks the mechanic.
The yard round now belongs to playtest::GrannysYardSession, not main.cpp.
